methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $1.7B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Kairos Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Late Stage)

data.narrative:
TerraPower is a late-stage nuclear energy company founded by Bill Gates that has raised $1.7B+ to date. The nuclear energy sector includes three public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and five identifiable private competitors (Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, Kairos Power), though the private peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables for meaningful multiples triangulation.

Given the limited peer data, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology applied to the $1.7B raised proxy. A 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of late-stage advancement yields an implied value of $2.0B. This represents a modest premium to capital deployed, consistent with the stage and sector risk profile. The narrow applicability of peer-median approaches—only one viable triangulation method—limits the robustness of the central estimate.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.7B to $2.3B, with $2.0B as the median point. Key uncertainties include the absence of recent late-stage nuclear energy financings sufficient for market multiples, and the heterogeneous technology approaches across the peer set, which limit direct comparability. Refinement would require access to recent private fundraising rounds or path-to-revenue milestones in the nuclear sector.
